title: Cursor pagination over (ts, id) with first-page/next-page branch
prompt: Starting from `test_fixtures/offset_pagination_slow.ex`, rewrite `MyApp.Blog.Feed.feed/1` as a cursor-paginated function `feed/1` that takes either `nil` (first page) or a `{published_at, id}` tuple (subsequent pages). Each page returns 20 rows ordered `(published_at desc, id desc)`. For subsequent pages use the row-wise comparison `(p.published_at < ^ts) or (p.published_at == ^ts and p.id < ^last_id)` — this is the deterministic cursor. Write to `lib/my_app/blog/feed.ex`.
tier_rationale: Full tuple-cursor rewrite with pattern-match branches and the non-obvious row-wise comparison. Sonnet commonly produces a broken single-column cursor.
research_citation: duffelhq/paginator: 'This method requires a deterministic sort order'
